description:
You are a log analyst who has just been given direct terminal access to a server.
    Two log files are already present on the machine:

        1. /home/user/logs/syslog
        2. /home/user/logs/access.log

    Your job is to inspect those logs and build two concise, machine-readable
    summary reports that will be used by an automated dashboard.

    STEP-BY-STEP GOAL
    -----------------
    1. Create the directory /home/user/analysis if it does not already exist.
    2. Read /home/user/logs/syslog and calculate, for every distinct “service”
       (the token that appears immediately before the PID in square brackets,
       e.g. “sshd”, “apache2”, “cron”, …):
          • the total number of lines whose message contains the word “ERROR”
          • the total number of lines whose message contains the word “WARNING”
    3. Save those counts in CSV format to
       /home/user/analysis/error_warning_summary.csv

       EXACT required CSV format:
           • File must contain exactly three comma-separated columns in
             this order: service,error_count,warning_count
           • The very first line MUST be the header line shown above.
           • Each subsequent line MUST contain the service name exactly as
             it appears in the log, then its error_count, then its
             warning_count.
           • Lines may appear in any order, but the file must finish with a
             single UNIX newline (<LF>).

       Example (illustrative only – do NOT hard-code numbers!):
           service,error_count,warning_count
           sshd,12,7
           apache2,4,2

    4. Read /home/user/logs/access.log and find the five IP addresses that occur
       most frequently (i.e. highest hit count).  If there is a tie for 5th
       place, choose the lexicographically smaller IPs until you have exactly
       five entries.

    5. Write those five addresses to /home/user/analysis/top_ips.log using the
       following plain-text format – ONE line per address:

           <IP> - <Hits>

       • “<IP>” must be the IP address string.
       • “<Hits>” must be the exact integer count.
       • The lines MUST be ordered in descending hit count; for equal counts,
         order lexicographically ascending by IP.
       • Do not include any extra lines before or after the list.
       • The file must end with a single UNIX newline (<LF>).

       Example (illustrative only – counts will differ!):
           203.0.113.2 - 14
           192.0.2.10  - 11
           10.0.0.25   - 11
           198.51.100.5- 9
           10.0.0.124  - 7

    6. When both files are ready, you are done.  No other output files are
       required.

    VERIFICATION
    ------------
    Automated tests will confirm:

        • /home/user/analysis/error_warning_summary.csv exists and matches the
          exact CSV requirements above.
        • /home/user/analysis/top_ips.log exists, contains exactly five lines,
          and follows the required format/order.
        • Counts and IP ordering are correct for the supplied log files.

    You do NOT need root access, and no system configuration changes are
    required.  Use only normal user-level commands and tools available in a
    standard Linux environment (e.g., cat, grep, awk, sort, uniq, etc.).
truth:
<!--
        The harness will create these two input log files **before** the agent
        begins.  Their full exact contents are listed here for grading and
        must not be revealed in the public task description above.
    -->

    <!-- syslog ground-truth -->
    <file path="/home/user/logs/syslog"><![CDATA[
Jan 12 10:15:23 host sshd[1024]: ERROR Failed password for invalid user admin from 192.168.1.10 port 52222 ssh2
Jan 12 10:16:45 host sshd[1026]: WARNING Authentication delayed for 192.168.1.11 port 52223
Jan 12 10:17:01 host apache2[2048]: ERROR mod_proxy: connect to backend failed
Jan 12 10:17:05 host apache2[2048]: WARNING mod_proxy: retrying request to backend
Jan 12 10:17:10 host sshd[1029]: ERROR Failed password for invalid user root from 192.168.1.12 port 52224 ssh2
Jan 12 10:18:20 host cron[3072]: WARNING Some cron job used deprecated feature
Jan 12 10:19:30 host sshd[1031]: WARNING Connection closed by 192.168.1.11 port 52223 [preauth]
Jan 12 10:20:31 host apache2[2050]: ERROR File does not exist: /var/www/favicon.ico
Jan 12 10:21:40 host apache2[2050]: ERROR client denied by server configuration: /var/www/secret
Jan 12 10:22:45 host sshd[1033]: ERROR Failed password for invalid user admin from 192.168.1.13 port 52226 ssh2
]]></file>

    <!-- Derived counts used for grading -->
    <expected_csv path="/home/user/analysis/error_warning_summary.csv"><![CDATA[
service,error_count,warning_count
sshd,3,2
apache2,3,1
cron,0,1
]]></expected_csv>

    <!-- access.log ground-truth -->
    <file path="/home/user/logs/access.log"><![CDATA[
192.168.1.10 - - [12/Jan/2023:10:15:23 +0000] "GET /index.html HTTP/1.1" 200 1024
192.168.1.11 - - [12/Jan/2023:10:15:25 +0000] "GET /contact.html HTTP/1.1" 200 512
10.0.0.5      - - [12/Jan/2023:10:15:27 +0000] "GET /index.html HTTP/1.1" 200 1024
192.168.1.12 - - [12/Jan/2023:10:15:29 +0000] "GET /about.html HTTP/1.1" 200 2048
192.168.1.10 - - [12/Jan/2023:10:15:33 +0000] "POST /login HTTP/1.1" 302 128
172.16.0.2   - - [12/Jan/2023:10:15:35 +0000] "GET /index.html HTTP/1.1" 200 1024
192.168.1.10 - - [12/Jan/2023:10:15:37 +0000] "GET /dashboard HTTP/1.1" 200 4096
192.168.1.11 - - [12/Jan/2023:10:15:39 +0000] "GET /pricing HTTP/1.1" 200 2048
192.168.1.10 - - [12/Jan/2023:10:15:41 +0000] "GET /reports HTTP/1.1" 200 2048
203.0.113.8  - - [12/Jan/2023:10:15:43 +0000] "GET /index.html HTTP/1.1" 404 256
10.0.0.5      - - [12/Jan/2023:10:15:45 +0000] "GET /docs HTTP/1.1" 200 1024
192.168.1.11 - - [12/Jan/2023:10:15:47 +0000] "POST /login HTTP/1.1" 302 128
192.168.1.10 - - [12/Jan/2023:10:15:49 +0000] "GET /settings HTTP/1.1" 200 2048
10.0.0.5      - - [12/Jan/2023:10:15:51 +0000] "GET /admin HTTP/1.1" 403 512
192.168.1.11 - - [12/Jan/2023:10:15:53 +0000] "GET /logout HTTP/1.1" 200 256
192.168.1.12 - - [12/Jan/2023:10:15:55 +0000] "GET /reports HTTP/1.1" 200 2048
172.16.0.2   - - [12/Jan/2023:10:15:57 +0000] "GET /faq HTTP/1.1" 200 1024
192.168.1.12 - - [12/Jan/2023:10:15:59 +0000] "GET /dashboard HTTP/1.1" 200 4096
]]></file>

    <!-- Derived top-IP list used for grading -->
    <expected_top_ips path="/home/user/analysis/top_ips.log"><![CDATA[
192.168.1.10 - 5
192.168.1.11 - 4
10.0.0.5 - 3
192.168.1.12 - 3
172.16.0.2 - 2
]]></expected_top_ips>

    <!--
        Both expected output files end with a single newline character.
        File permissions: the harness will ensure /home/user/analysis is
        writable by the user so the agent can create the files.
    -->
